evals.prompt: I just set up table permissions for my e-commerce site but I'm not sure if I got everything right. Can you audit the permissions and let me know if there are any issues? I have tables for Products, Orders, Order Items, and Customers.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads existing web roles and table permissions from .powerpages-site/, analyzes the site code for /_api/ patterns, @odata.bind lookups, and file upload patterns, checks .datamodel-manifest.json for the authoritative table list, queries Dataverse for lookup columns using query-table-lookups.js, runs all audit checks, generates an HTML audit report using the template with findings sorted by severity, displays it in the browser, records skill usage, and presents a summary asking if the user wants to fix issues.
expectations: Task tracking is set up at Step 1 with TaskCreate for all 6 tasks (Verify site deployment, Gather configuration, Discover relationships, Run audit checks, Generate audit report, Present findings)
Site deployment is verified by checking .powerpages-site folder and table permissions exist
All existing web roles are read from .powerpages-site/web-roles/
All existing table permissions are read from .powerpages-site/table-permissions/
Site source code is searched for /_api/ patterns to identify tables used in code
Site source code is searched for @odata.bind patterns to identify lookup relationships
Site source code is searched for file upload patterns (uploadFileColumn, uploadFile, etc.)
.datamodel-manifest.json is checked for the authoritative table list
pac env who is run to get the environment URL for Dataverse API calls
query-table-lookups.js script is used to query Dataverse for lookup columns on tables with create/write permissions
Missing permissions are flagged as critical for tables referenced in code but lacking permissions
Missing append/appendto flags are checked for tables with lookup relationships
Overly broad Global scope with write/delete is flagged as warning
HTML audit report is generated using the template from assets/audit-report.html with placeholder replacement
Health score is calculated based on findings (critical: -15, warning: -5, info: -1)
Report is saved to docs/permissions-audit.html in the project directory
Report is opened in browser via Playwright with 1920x1080 resize and full-page screenshot taken
Skill usage is recorded via skill tracking reference
Summary is presented with health score, critical/warning counts, and offer to fix issues
Each task is marked in_progress when starting and completed when done

evals.prompt: audit my power pages permissions. i think something is wrong because users are getting 403 errors when trying to create orders. the site has products, orders, and order items tables
evals.expected_output: The skill should identify likely causes of 403 errors — most commonly missing append/appendto for lookup relationships or missing create permissions. The audit uses query-table-lookups.js to discover lookup columns and flags missing append/appendto as critical findings with reasoning about why they cause 403 errors and specific fix suggestions.
expectations: Task tracking is used throughout all 6 tasks
Existing permissions are discovered from .powerpages-site/table-permissions/
Code analysis identifies which CRUD operations are performed on each table
pac env who is run to get the environment URL
query-table-lookups.js is used to discover lookup columns on tables with create/write permissions
Missing append flag is detected if orders have lookups to products without append: true
Missing appendto flag is detected if products are referenced by order lookups without appendto: true
Missing create permission is flagged if no permission grants create on the orders table
Findings specifically reference the association/disassociation error message in reasoning
HTML report includes critical findings at the top of the findings list
Suggested fixes include exact permission changes needed (enable append, appendto, or create)
Report health score reflects the severity of found issues
Skill usage is recorded via skill tracking reference
User is asked if they want the issues fixed after presenting the report

evals.prompt: can you do a security review of my table permissions? I want to make sure we're following least-privilege principles. we have a portal with case management -- cases, notes, attachments, contacts, and knowledge articles
evals.expected_output: The skill should focus on scope analysis — checking for overly permissive Global scope where Contact or Parent would be safer, validating parent-child chains for notes/attachments inheriting from cases using query-table-relationships.js, and flagging any Global write/delete permissions. Knowledge articles with Global read-only should pass as appropriate for public content.
expectations: Task tracking is used with TaskCreate at the start for all 6 tasks
All table permissions are inventoried
Parent chain integrity is verified for notes and attachments inheriting from cases
query-table-relationships.js is used to validate parentrelationshipname values for parent-scope permissions
Contact scope is validated as appropriate for case records (users see only own cases)
Global scope with read-only for knowledge articles is marked as pass (appropriate for public content)
Global scope with write or delete is flagged as warning with reasoning about least-privilege
Write-without-read is flagged if any permission has write: true but read: false
Orphaned permissions are detected for tables not referenced in site code
Missing web role associations are flagged as warning
HTML report is generated with correct health score
Inventory data is included in the report with all permissions in a clean table
Findings include both issues and passed checks to show thoroughness
If Dataverse API scripts fail, those checks are skipped and noted in the report rather than stopping the audit

evals.prompt: check my permissions setup. this is a brand new site and I haven't configured any table permissions yet, but I already have web roles and my site code references several tables via the Web API
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect that no table permissions exist at all but tables are used in code, flagging each missing permission as critical. The report should have a low health score and multiple critical findings, one for each table referenced in code.
expectations: Site deployment is verified (.powerpages-site exists)
The absence of any table permissions is detected and noted as a critical finding
Web roles are still read and noted as existing
Site code is analyzed to identify which tables need permissions via /_api/ and @odata.bind patterns
Each table referenced in code gets a critical finding for missing permission
Findings include reasoning about which code files reference each table
Suggested fixes recommend appropriate scope and CRUD flags based on code analysis
Health score is very low (near 0) due to multiple critical findings
No passed checks for permission configuration (since none exist)
HTML report is generated using the template and displayed via Playwright
Skill usage is recorded via skill tracking reference
User is offered to fix all issues, potentially suggesting /power-pages:setup-permissions for a complete permissions plan
